evidence.scientific_clarity: Forest plot clearly shows 12 studies with CI lines, square markers sized by weight, diamond pooled estimate, null-effect vertical line, and labeled columns for study, effect [95% CI], and weight.
evidence.typography: Script sets no pdf.fonttype=42 or svg.fonttype=0; PDF uses Type3 (bitmap-outlined) DejaVuSans fonts, which are not editable/selectable in Illustrator/Inkscape; DejaVu Sans is the matplotlib default and not a preferred publication font.
evidence.palette: CI lines and squares use neutral #333333/#1f77b4 (acceptable); pooled diamond uses red #d62728 which is directionally appropriate for a summary estimate; no colorblind-problematic red/green pairing, but blue default is matplotlib standard rather than a deliberate CB-safe choice.
evidence.layout_density: Panel fills the 9x7 figure well; study labels on left, effect values and weights on right; y-range covers all rows with small padding; no overlapping labels observed.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches figure content precisely; legend-type information is embedded as column headers; no legend box needed; minor issue: null line described as 'dashed' in caption but script draws it as solid (ax.axvline linestyle='-').
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF uses Type3 fonts (outline-as-path bitmaps), not Type1 or TrueType with fonttype=42; text is not selectable in PDF viewers; script lacks 'matplotlib.rcParams["pdf.fonttype"]=42'.
evidence.file_format: All four required files present and non-trivial: figure.pdf (29K), figure.png (83K), caption.tex (434B), gen_figure.py (3.6K); bonus figure.svg also present.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption states what is shown (log OR forest plot, 12 studies), describes encoding (square size ~ weight, CI lines), names the pooled method (inverse-variance fixed-effects), and includes specific numerical claim (theta-hat=0.245, 95% CI [0.196, 0.294]).

overall_strengths: Well-structured forest plot with all required elements: study markers, CI lines, diamond pooled estimate, null-effect line, and annotated numerical values with weights.
Caption is detailed and quantitative, explicitly reporting the pooled estimate and CI so readers can verify the main claim without re-reading the figure.
overall_weaknesses: PDF font embedding uses Type3 (bitmapped outlines) because pdf.fonttype=42 is not set, making text non-selectable and unsuitable for camera-ready journal submission.
Minor caption/figure mismatch: caption calls the null-effect line 'dashed' but the script draws it solid (linestyle='-'), which Reviewer 2 would flag.
